title: Aboulker et al. Conjecture (→←← is χ-bounding)
statement_text: The oriented three-edge path $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$ is $\chi$-bounding, i.e., there exists a function $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for every oriented graph $G$ containing no induced copy of $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$.
context_text: Kierstead and Rödl [12] first asked whether $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$ is $\chi$-bounding and showed the class with no induced $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$ and no cyclic triangle is $\chi$-bounded. Aboulker et al. [1] subsequently conjectured the full affirmative answer and proved further special cases. The remaining two orientations of the three-edge path ($\to\to\to$ and $\to\leftarrow\to$) were already known to be non-$\chi$-bounding.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.1: the oriented paths $\to\leftarrow\leftarrow$ and $\leftarrow\to\to$ are $\chi$-bounding, fully resolving the conjecture.
notes: PDF source; arrow notation →←← is ASCII representation of the orientation of a three-edge path with both outer edges directed towards the interior. Kierstead–Rödl [12] originally posed the question; Aboulker et al. [1] elevated it to a conjecture.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. XI. Orientations

title: Aboulker et al. Conjecture (every oriented star is χ-bounding)
statement_text: Every oriented star is $\chi$-bounding, i.e., for every oriented star $H$ there exists a function $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for every oriented graph $G$ containing no induced copy of $H$.
context_text: Gyárfás [8] asked whether every oriented star $H$ gives a $\chi$-bounded class of acyclic oriented graphs with no induced $H$. Kierstead and Rödl [12] proved the stronger result that the class with no induced $H$ and no cyclic triangle is $\chi$-bounded, and asked the full question. Aboulker et al. [1] conjectured the affirmative and showed the result when additionally no transitive triangle is present.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.2: every oriented star is $\chi$-bounding, fully resolving the conjecture; as a corollary, if $F$ is a forest all of whose components are stars then every orientation of $F$ is $\chi$-bounding.
notes: PDF source; Kierstead–Rödl [12] originally asked the question; Aboulker et al. [1] elevated it to a conjecture. The easy cases (all edges directed away from or all towards the centre) were known; the mixed-direction case is the main difficulty.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. XI. Orientations
